title: Alchemy Infrastructure For Vercel And Photon Tasks
principles: Adopt before mutate: an existing resource is Unowned until an exact reviewed manifest authorizes adoption.
Alchemy owns stable configuration and drift; Vercel Git and app-owned runbooks own deployment, promotion, rollback, and runtime proof.
Preview and Production have separate state, credentials, manifests, storage namespaces, and Photon projects; no shared Photon project is accepted as an isolation fallback.
Provider input is decoded once, output is decoded immediately, secrets and personal/channel data never enter state or ordinary proof, and every custom boundary has live and memory Layers.
Production projects, domains, data stores, Photon project/users/webhooks, and bindings begin retained and delete-protected.
riskLenses: Ownership and call graph: root stack topology, private infrastructure tooling, existing Photon management ownership, app runtime boundaries, Vercel Git deployment ownership, and runbook authority remain distinct.

Implementation quality: canonical Schema Type/Encoded contracts, branded identities, Config.schema with Redacted secrets, named services, safe tagged errors, flat sequential Effects, immediate decode/encode, explicit live/memory Layers, and no raw clients, primitive semantic strings, generic callbacks, unsafe casts, or helper sprawl.

Verification coverage: provider fakes and Alchemy lifecycle tests cover import, adoption, no-op, drift, create/update/replace/delete, timeout-after-write, partial failure, retain/delete protection, secret leakage, provider readback, deployment proof, and separately bounded Channel/handset proof.

tasks.title: Prove the Alchemy and Effect foundation without provider access
tasks.description: Pin the revalidated Alchemy v2 package, add the private @bundjil/infrastructure package and root stack router, and prove one synthetic resource through the complete Schema, service, Layer, Resource, Provider, state, plan, adoption, retention, and test-provider path. Use ignored local state only.
implementationNotes: Add alchemy.run.ts, stacks/bundjil.ts, and the package structure defined by the SPEC. Applications must not import @bundjil/infrastructure.
Define InfrastructureStage, logical/physical identity, SecretReference, deletion policy, adoption manifest digest, safe result, and tagged error Schemas with explicit Type and Encoded exports.
Load stack/stage/mode and synthetic provider config with Config.schema; use Redacted for every credential-shaped value.

Follow the site pattern at /Users/cooper/Projects/site/alchemy.run.ts for Alchemy.Stack, Stage-dependent Provider Layers, state, flat Effect.gen composition, and schema-shaped outputs without copying site-specific resources or state identity.
Implement a memory service and synthetic custom resource that can return missing, Unowned, no-op, drift, replacement, OutcomeUncertain, retain, and delete-protected results.
mandatoryVerification: Alchemy provider-harness tests cover create, read, no-op, in-place update, replacement, list, refresh, adoption denied/allowed, retain, delete protection, timeout-after-write, and state-write partial failure.
Leak tests prove secret and personal-data sentinels are absent from state serialization, plan, outputs, errors, spans, and receipts.
Run all global repository-only checks that are available after the package is added; no provider credential, network request, or remote state is used.
Record all three risk lenses and confirm no raw client, generic callback, primitive semantic ID, instanceof policy, unchecked output, manual DTO reader, or common/utils/helper sprawl.
completionCriteria: A deterministic local plan and provider test prove the intended lifecycle contract without touching Vercel, Photon, DNS, secrets, webhooks, deployments, or remote state.
The infrastructure package has a narrow README, exports only owned contracts, and remains outside app runtime dependency graphs.

tasks.title: Build the Vercel read and import vertical slice
tasks.description: Implement the private Vercel Effect client and custom read/import resources for projects, project settings, domains, environment metadata, Marketplace bindings, and deployment observations. Live writes remain disabled.
implementationNotes: Keep Vercel HTTP details private under @bundjil/infrastructure/vercel; do not create @bundjil/vercel without a second proved consumer.
Expose named VercelProjects, VercelDomains, VercelEnvironmentVariables, VercelMarketplaceBindings, and VercelDeployments operations with operation-specific tagged errors and live/memory Layers.
Decode every unknown status/body/header response immediately. Brand team, project, env, domain, integration, resource, database, and deployment identities.
Implement full pagination, team scoping, rate-header handling, exact missing/ambiguous classification, sensitive env metadata, and read-only deployment provenance.
Resources return Unowned for exact existing matches and reject create/update/delete while read-only mode is active.
mandatoryVerification: Contract tests cover valid and malformed fixtures, pagination, 404, ambiguous names, team mismatch, 429/reset headers, transient 5xx, sensitive env write-only metadata, Marketplace identity, and immutable deployment observations.
Provider tests prove import/adoption planning without writes and reject project/domain/database/deployment deletion.
State/plan/error leak tests cover Vercel token, sensitive env value, integration credential, URL query, and raw body sentinels.
Run global checks, package typecheck/tests, and Effect language-service diagnostics for all changed TypeScript.
completionCriteria: A complete synthetic two-project inventory can be planned and adopted from memory fixtures by stable IDs with zero provider writes.
Vercel Git deployment and promotion operations are absent from convergent resource create/update/delete paths.

tasks.title: Build the Photon read and import vertical slice
tasks.description: Harden the existing @bundjil/photon management boundary for Alchemy consumers and implement read/import resources for project observation, iMessage platform, shared users, webhooks, line inventory, and billing observation. No Photon write is performed.
implementationNotes: Expose a narrow @bundjil/photon/management subpath only after replacing proof-specific generic errors with operation-specific safe contracts where required. Keep HttpClient, auth, URLs, DTOs, and raw provider errors private.

Reuse existing PhotonProjectId, PhotonUserId, PhotonWebhookId, project-secret, shared-user, and platform Schemas; add line/billing identities and response Schemas only where primary API contracts require them.
Keep phone and assigned-number semantics Redacted and out of Alchemy attributes/receipts. The physical shared-user identity is the stable user UUID.
Return Unowned for exact reviewed project/platform/user/webhook matches. Assert Free shared service and zero dedicated lines without mutating them.
Represent webhook signing state only by external reference/revision and secretConfigured; list cannot recover the signing secret.
mandatoryVerification: Contract tests cover platform/user/webhook/line/billing success, malformed output, pagination, semantic user ambiguity, same-URL webhook ambiguity, 404, 409, 429, transient 5xx, and unavailable billing readback.
Provider tests prove project/line/billing read-only behavior, exact stable-ID adoption, and rejection of project, dedicated-line, billing, or secret-rotation writes.
Leak tests cover project secret, signing secret, phone, assigned number, webhook URL query, raw response, Space/message identity, and SDK error sentinels.
Run @bundjil/photon and @bundjil/infrastructure typechecks/tests plus every global repository gate.
completionCriteria: The complete accepted Photon topology can be represented from memory fixtures without duplicating management HTTP logic or importing runtime Spectrum operations into Alchemy.
Project, line, billing, and create-only secret gaps are explicit and fail closed.

tasks.title: Capture an authorized read-only Vercel and Photon inventory
tasks.description: Under new target-specific read authority, run metadata-only Vercel and Photon inventory for Preview and Production, resolve exact physical identities and isolation, and produce sanitized manifests. Do not create, update, delete, rotate, deploy, promote, or send.
implementationNotes: Resolve the current authenticated principal, Vercel team/projects, environment targets/types, domains, integration/configuration/resource/database IDs, deployment/protection metadata, Photon project/platform/service/users/webhooks/lines/billing, and current Preview/Production credential ownership.
Use the target-owned Vercel and Photon runbooks and stop on identity, scope, permission, ambiguity, unavailable readback, or secret-output risk.
Do not print or retain sensitive values, phone numbers, assigned numbers, webhook URLs with proof queries, message identities, account data, or credentials.
Record whether the current shared Photon project can be replaced by a separate Free Preview project; observation does not authorize project creation.
Generate canonical observed-manifest hashes and explicit adopted-versus-rollout-created classifications where evidence exists.
mandatoryVerification: Readback receipts bind principal, target, stage, operation, observedAt, exact safe physical IDs/fingerprints, source SHA, limitations, non-claims, and zero writes.
A second inventory read produces the same canonical manifest or a classified drift result.
Provider logs and local artifacts pass leak scans; every unavailable field remains unknown rather than inferred.
Repository verification passes before the inventory evidence is accepted.
completionCriteria: Preview and Production resource inventories are complete enough to build exact adoption manifests or explicitly list the unresolved readbacks that block adoption.
No provider or deployment mutation occurred, and historical receipts were not promoted to current truth.

tasks.title: Bootstrap state, secret custody, and no-op adoption
tasks.description: Choose and separately authorize a dedicated remote Alchemy state backend, implement the named secret-custody boundary, and adopt existing Preview then Production resources one class at a time with provider writes disabled.
implementationNotes: If Cloudflare.state() is chosen, use a Bundjil-specific stack/backend identity and scoped credentials; never reuse the site state or token.
Implement SecretReference resolution and PhotonWebhookSecretCustody as named services with live/memory Layers. State stores references/revisions/booleans only.
Create reviewed adoption manifests from the accepted inventory, including logical ID, physical ID, stage, deletion policy, observed-manifest digest, and owner.
Adopt Vercel projects, domains, env metadata, Marketplace/Upstash observations, Photon project/platform/user/webhooks, line/billing observations, and deployment observations in that order.
Stop on any create, replacement, delete, provider write, secret value in state, or changed manifest hash.
mandatoryVerification: Two consecutive refreshed plans are no-op at the same provider snapshot for Preview and Production.
Adoption tests reject wrong stage, wrong physical ID, changed observed digest, renamed logical ID, missing retain policy, and any secret-bearing state.
Remote-state rollback and access-revocation procedures are dry-run validated without destroying state.
Run full repository verification and record repository versus provider versus state proof separately.
completionCriteria: Existing resources are represented in remote state without provider mutation, accidental replacement, deletion, secret disclosure, or traffic change.
Every Production project/domain/store/Photon resource is retained and delete-protected.

tasks.title: Prove convergent Vercel configuration in Preview
tasks.description: Under separately approved Preview write authority, enable the smallest reversible Vercel project-setting and environment-metadata resource, observe convergence, induce safe drift, reconcile it, and prove that deployment remains owned by Vercel Git.
implementationNotes: Select non-secret Preview-only configuration with a recorded before value and safe rollback. Do not begin with Production, domains, Marketplace, auth tokens, or provider secrets.
Plan, approve, apply, refresh, induce one safe manual drift, detect it, reconcile once, and restore the accepted state.
Use Vercel Git to create the resulting immutable Preview deployment; Alchemy may observe but must not create/promote it.
Prove project/team/target identity before every operation and revoke write credentials after the bounded spike.
Capture latency, API attempts, plan readability, drift precision, recovery effort, and operator attention for the effectiveness scorecard.
mandatoryVerification: Provider tests for the exercised resource already cover no-op, update, timeout-after-write, eventual consistency, retain, and rollback.
Authenticated readback proves the exact Preview setting converged and Production was untouched.
Deployment observation binds the resulting Vercel deployment to the immutable Git SHA and records no promotion.
The spike passes the SPEC effectiveness bar: deterministic second no-op, no secret/state leak, bounded recovery, and clearer diff than the current imperative procedure.
completionCriteria: Alchemy safely detects and reconciles one real Preview Vercel drift while Vercel Git remains the only deployment creator.
Rollback restores the prior setting and readback, with no Production or data-resource change.

tasks.title: Prove isolated Photon Preview reconciliation
tasks.description: Only after a separate Photon Preview project and secret-custody path are approved, adopt or create one synthetic Free shared user and one Preview webhook, prove timeout/partial-failure recovery and signed ingress, then exercise retain-safe cleanup. If project isolation is unavailable, record the spike as blocked and perform no shared-project write.
implementationNotes: Require a separate Preview project, Free shared service, zero dedicated lines, isolated credentials, synthetic approved user, Preview callback, and rollback identities.
Enable only iMessage if needed, reconcile the shared user using documented semantic idempotency, and register one webhook while persisting the create-only secret through PhotonWebhookSecretCustody.
Test observe-after-timeout without blind create replay and preserve any uncertain endpoint by stable ID/URL.
Bind the secret reference to the Preview Vercel environment, let Vercel Git deploy, then follow the Photon runbook for signed ingress and bounded Channel proof.
Never create/delete a dedicated line, mutate billing, reuse Production user/credential/namespace, or send outside the approved proof conversation.
mandatoryVerification: Authenticated management readback proves project, service, platform, stable user/webhook IDs, zero dedicated lines, and separate Preview credentials without exposing phone or secret values.
A forced memory-provider timeout-after-write and state-partial-failure test recovers by observation and never creates a duplicate.
Deployment and Channel receipts separately prove source/deployment identity, signed ingress, replay disposition, provider acceptance, limitations, and no Production inference.
Cleanup deletes only rollout-created resources when explicitly authorized, waits through the retry horizon, and preserves adopted resources.
completionCriteria: The isolated Preview Photon lifecycle converges and recovers without duplicate users/webhooks or secret leakage, or the task records a truthful blocked result with zero shared-project mutation.
No Production Photon, Sendblue, DNS, line, billing, or unrelated user state changed.

tasks.title: Adopt stable env, Marketplace, storage, and inter-service bindings
tasks.description: After the Preview spikes pass, enable approved env metadata/value-revision ownership, exact Marketplace/Upstash binding observation, coordinated agent-to-proxy auth revision, and deployment observations without creating data stores or promoting deployments.
implementationNotes: Derive the exact env inventory from every Config owner and Vercel readback; remove no key based only on a stale README or alias.
Resolve secret values at apply time and compare only SecretReference revisions. Coordinate agent/proxy internal token changes as one rotation group.
Import Marketplace configuration/resource/database IDs and project/target attachments. Never create or detach a database from an env alias.
Preserve Channel replay and Codex profile prefixes/data. Reject Preview/Production credential, prefix, project, or database crossover.
Require a new immutable Vercel deployment after env changes and observe it; promotion remains runbook-owned.
mandatoryVerification: Memory tests cover secret revision no-op/update, two-project coordinated rotation, partial one-project failure, deployment-required result, Marketplace ambiguity, and data-store retain.
Preview readback proves exact key/type/target metadata and safe revision fingerprints; secret values remain absent.
Profile/replay continuity checks prove namespaces and existing data identities were preserved without reading application data unnecessarily.
Full repository verification and all three risk-lens audits pass.
completionCriteria: Stable bindings and auth revisions converge in Preview with exact provider readback, immutable deployment observation, and no data-store recreation or promotion.
Rollback retains both credential revisions until the old deployment remains usable.

tasks.title: Apply the accepted stable configuration to Production
tasks.description: Under new Production authority, run a zero-replacement plan, apply only previously accepted stable configuration classes, refresh provider state, let Vercel build a Production candidate, and use app-owned runbooks for proof and promotion.
implementationNotes: Bind immutable main SHA, state revision, adoption manifest, Vercel/Photon principals, current rollback deployments, and approved operation list.
Reject project/domain/store/webhook/user replacement, any delete, Photon project/line/billing mutation, DNS write, secret rotation outside an approved group, or ambiguous readback.
Apply in dependency order: proxy stable config, agent-to-proxy bindings, agent Channel/storage config, Photon platform/user/webhook only if already proved and explicitly approved.
Refresh and require no drift before Vercel deployment proof. Promote proxy before agent for URL/auth changes.
Retain previous deployments, env revisions, Photon webhook, users, projects, stores, and state until rollback expiry.
mandatoryVerification: The approved Production plan contains no unapproved create, replacement, delete, database operation, domain move, Photon project/line/billing change, or secret disclosure.
Provider readback proves the exact stable resources; Vercel deployment proof binds source/config; app runbooks separately prove health/auth/Channel behavior and promotion.
A failed/uncertain apply stops promotion and records safe recovery without blind retry.
Production and Preview state, credentials, projects, namespaces, manifests, and receipts remain distinct.
completionCriteria: Production stable configuration is Alchemy-owned by exact adopted IDs and refreshed no-op state, while deployment/promotion remains Vercel Git/runbook-owned.
Rollback readiness is proved and no evidence class is overstated.

tasks.title: Complete drift CI, monitoring, documentation, and the five-pass audit
tasks.description: Add report-only refreshed plan workflows, bounded observability and alerts for owned failure classes, close every documentation/authority/proof owner, and perform the required five-pass audit after all implementation tasks are complete.
implementationNotes: PR CI runs repository checks, provider-memory tests, and read-only Preview plan. Protected Preview/Production apply jobs remain separate and approval-gated.
Scheduled drift uses read-only credentials, produces sanitized classified findings, and never auto-applies. Route any future automation through controls admission.
Emit safe Effect spans/receipts for stage, resource kind, operation, physical-ID fingerprint, attempts, duration, diff, retry, and known/uncertain outcome.
Update architecture, package/root READMEs, runbooks, authority/control registers, verification contracts, proof templates, current plan, and supporting research at their earliest owners.

Perform prd-implementer's five passes only after task code is complete: implementation correctness, Effect/Schema/service/Layer quality, test and failure-path coverage, docs/authority/proof consistency, and final clean verification/Git state.
mandatoryVerification: Drift fixtures cover expected normalization, unowned resource, in-place drift, destructive drift, unavailable/ambiguous readback, unknown secret revision, and deployment drift.
Workflow policy proves PR jobs cannot apply, Preview cannot target Production, scheduled drift is report-only, and protected apply requires exact authority.
The five-pass audit reports findings and corrections for every risk lens, including no generic helper/client sprawl and no secret/personal-data leaks.
Run every global verification command, all package/provider tests, Alchemy provider tests, scoped runbook dry runs, git diff --check, and exact Git identity/readback.
completionCriteria: All acceptance criteria in the SPEC are met or explicitly blocked with retained evidence and no false completion claim.
The final tree has complete docs-maintainer decisions, accepted verification, rollback identity, and no unauthorized provider operation.
